q: Consider a hash table of size 7 with hash function $h(k) = k \bmod 7$. Using linear probing, insert keys 10, 24, 42, 17, 31 into the table. What is the index of key 31?
subject: Computer Science & IT
expl: $10 \bmod 7 = 3$, $24 \bmod 7 = 3 \to$ probes to index 4. $42 \bmod 7 = 0$. $17 \bmod 7 = 3 \to$ probes to 5. $31 \bmod 7 = 3 \to$ probes 3, 4, 5 $\to$ lands at index 6.

q: Which of the following scheduling algorithms is non-preemptive?
subject: Computer Science & IT
expl: FCFS processes requests strictly in arrival order until completion without preemption.

q: The worst-case time complexity of QuickSort on an array of $n$ elements is:
subject: Computer Science & IT
expl: Worst case occurs when the pivot chosen is always the smallest or largest element (e.g. already sorted array), yielding $T(n) = T(n-1) + \mathcal{O}(n) = \mathcal{O}(n^2)$.
